conbody.p: This section describes the new features in the Juniper Cloud-Native Router (JCNR) 24.3 release.

section.title: New Features in Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 24.3

li.p: Support for Topology-Independent Loop-Free Alternates (TI-LFA) with fast reroute (FRR)—JCNR supports implementing Topology-Independent Loop-Free Alternates (TI-LFA) with fast reroute (FRR) in SR-MPLS. TI-LFA is supported on IS-IS implementations for SR-MPLS. It is also supported on OSPF implementations as a technology preview feature. Only protection against link failure is supported with one primary and one secondary (backup) path.

Support for Layer-3 Class of Service (CoS)—Juniper Cloud-Native Router supports Layer 3 Class of Service (CoS), also known as L3 Quality of Service (QoS). JCNR supports the following classifiers, policers and rewrite/marker implementations:
Single-rate three-color marker (srTCM) and Two-rate three-color marker (trTCM) policers
Rewrite rules supports copying outer IPv4/IPv6 DSCP marking to inner IP header DSCP field as well as MPLS EXP bit marking

Additional support for L3/L4 Access Lists—JCNR now supports MPLS protocol family for L3/L4 access lists. It also supports additional match nonterminating actions including syslog, routing-instance, forwarding class and policer.
Additional Telemetry Support—JCNR now supports telemetry for interfaces extended statistics, firewall filter counters and TI-LFA statistics via Prometheus and gNMI.

Support for Readiness Check during JCNR installation—JCNR now supports readiness preflight and postflight checks during installation. Preflight checks verify that your infrastructure can support JCNR and take place before JCNR is installed. Postflight checks verify that your JCNR installation is working properly and take place after JCNR is installed.
Support for Ubuntu OS—JCNR now supports Ubuntu OS as a supported operating system for installation on bare metal servers.
Support for configuring cRPD using CLI or NETCONF—JCNR supports configuring cRPD via CLI or NETCONF. The flag enableLocalPersistence must be enabled in the helm chart.
